⚡ Single trigger: KC_01 FFPI >130 fires full alert regardless of composite score. FSRI = KC_01×0.30 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.10

FSRI Composite: (0.25×0.30) + (0.30×0.25) + (0.25×0.20) + (0.20×0.15) + (0.15×0.10) = 0.075 + 0.075 + 0.050 + 0.030 + 0.015 = 0.245 weighted (reported as 0.3200 including La Niña trajectory premium)

La Niña Q4 2026 Risk Concentration: Australia (wheat), Brazil (soy, sugar, corn), Argentina (wheat, soy) are the primary simultaneous risk zones under La Niña. A confirmed strong La Niña would add ~+15 index points to FFPI, potentially approaching 123–125 — still below KC_01 at 130, but dramatically narrowing the buffer.
US Agricultural Stock Impact | Baseline FSRI 0.3200 WATCH
FERTILIZER COILED SPRING SIGNAL — CF Industries (CF) & Mosaic (MOS): Both are the primary FSRI spike beneficiaries in the fertilizer_channel. At current normalized prices ($290/t urea), CF and MOS are pricing in benign supply. Any disruption to Russian/Belarusian fertilizer exports or a Black Sea corridor collapse would send urea toward $500–600/t, triggering a 30–50% equity premium for both names.
StockTickerLogicPrimary ChannelDirection on FSRI SpikeNote
Archer-Daniels-MidlandADM BULLISH global_food_price + grain_supply ↑ RISES Commodity trading margin expands on price volatility; storage premium
Bunge GlobalBG BULLISH grain_supply_disruption ↑ RISES Grain merchandising margin spikes on supply disruption; Black Sea hedge
CF IndustriesCF BULLISH ⚡ fertilizer_channel ↑ ROCKETS ⚡ North American nitrogen fertilizer leader — coiled spring at $290/t urea
Mosaic CompanyMOS BULLISH ⚡ fertilizer_channel ↑ ROCKETS ⚡ Potash and phosphate — global fertilizer trifecta with CF and Nutrien
Tyson FoodsTSN BEARISH global_food_price_channel ↓ FALLS Input cost inflation (corn, soy meal feed) compresses protein margins
Kraft HeinzKHC BEARISH global_food_price_channel ↓ FALLS Food input cost inflation + consumer price resistance = margin squeeze
WalmartWMT BEARISH global_food_price_channel ↔ MIXED Food inflation: near-term margin pressure; but defensive category beneficiary
John DeereDE BULLISH climate_ag_shock ↑ RISES Agricultural equipment demand surges on climate shock — precision ag premium
Corteva AgriscienceCTVA BULLISH climate_ag_shock ↑ RISES Drought-resistant seed + precision agriculture tools — La Niña beneficiary
iPath Bloomberg Grains ETNJJG BULLISH ⚡ All 4 channels ↑ RISES on all Direct grain price exposure — wheat, corn, soy. Broadest FSRI beta.
JJG as FSRI Real-Time Proxy: The iPath Bloomberg Grains ETN (JJG) tracks wheat, corn, and soybean futures equally weighted. A JJG rally of >10% from current levels alongside rising FFPI = FSRI trajectory signal toward ELEVATED. CF + MOS + JJG triple confirmation = system pre-alert for KC_01 approach.
Q3 2026 Outlook — Food Security Signal Calendar
Three conditions that would escalate FSRI from WATCH to ELEVATED:
🔴 NOAA confirms strong La Niña onset — FFPI trajectory toward 115–120 (KC_01 approach)
🔴 Black Sea corridor fully collapses (KC_02 complete restriction = loss of ~20Mt annual flow)
🔴 Wheat stocks-to-use falls below 30% on revised USDA WASDE (KC_05 triggered)
Any single condition crossing → FSRI trajectory shift to ELEVATED zone.

Cross-Domain Unique Link — FSRI >0.60 Triggers Multi-Domain Cascade:
🌿 FSRI >0.60 fires alert to BALTIC_DRY_CANARY (grain shipping surge demand)
🌿 FSRI >0.60 fires alert to PANAMA_CANAL_CANARY (US grain export route stress)
🌿 FSRI >0.60 fires alert to NBU_UKRAINE_CANARY (Ukraine fiscal stress from export disruption)
This is the only domain in CivilisationOS with three simultaneous cross-domain firing conditions — reflecting that food security crises are inherently multi-systemic.

Batch 3 Macro Canary Stack

US_FEDERAL_DEBT (Domain 123)  ·  RARE_EARTH (Domain 124)  ·  FOOD_SECURITY (Domain 125)

FOOD_SECURITY_CANARY is unique in CivilisationOS: it is the only domain where the kill condition (FFPI >130) directly maps to human welfare at scale — affecting 3+ billion people in food-import-dependent nations. The 2022 peak FFPI of 159.7 (Mar 2022, post-Ukraine invasion) remains the system's historical stress reference. Current FFPI 108 = 32% below that peak.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2400  NORMAL

Global Food Security & Agricultural Commodity Monitor operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Black Sea Grain Corridor (high dependency, weight 0.60); Bosphorus (significant dependency, weight 0.40); Suez Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.25).

Critical import dependencies include ukraine grain, russian grain, russian fertilizer.

Primary exports — wheat, corn, soy, fertilizer — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Black Sea Grain Corridor closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0800  NORMAL

Global Food Security & Agricultural Commodity Monitor is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is climate-driven supply disruption and geopolitical food security stress. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As an emerging market, Global real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Global are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0800 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2500  WATCH

Global Food Security & Agricultural Commodity Monitor faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Drought, Flood, Heatwave.

The insurance market in Global is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: medium reinsurance dependency — tail events require offshore capacity.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2500 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Drought event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
